steps.title: Identify the interrogative structure
steps.content: The sentence opens with “Where,” marking this as an interrogative sentence. In questions formed with interrogative words, English requires subject-verb inversion: the auxiliary verb must precede the subject.

steps.title: Apply the inversion rule
steps.content:
Choice C correctly inverts the order (“did” before “the word”) and uses a question mark, forming a proper interrogative.

Choice A has the right word order but uses a period instead of a question mark.

Choice B fails the inversion by placing “the word” before “did.” Choice D omits the auxiliary entirely.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
